query: Build a settings dashboard with dark and light mode support. It should have a sidebar nav, a main content area with form controls, and a theme toggle. Use Tailwind and React. The design should feel premium in both themes.
assertions: Announces it is using the frontend-design skill
Provides specific dark mode color adaptation guidance (not just 'invert the colors')
Defines semantic color tokens that work across both themes (e.g., surface, on-surface, muted)
Addresses contrast requirements for dark backgrounds (light text at 4.5:1+ ratio)
Specifies how shadows, borders, and elevation change between light and dark modes
Does NOT use pure black (#000) as the dark mode background — uses near-black with tint
Covers the theme toggle implementation approach (CSS variables, class toggle, or Tailwind dark:)

query: Design a pricing page with three tiers. I want it to feel alive — polished micro-interactions on hover, focus, and when toggling between monthly/annual billing. The cards should respond to user interaction. Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, single file.
assertions: Announces it is using the frontend-design skill
Provides specific hover interaction recipes (not just 'add a hover effect')
Includes focus state styling with visible focus rings for keyboard navigation
Provides a toggle animation pattern for the monthly/annual billing switch
Specifies transition timing, easing, and properties for each interaction
Uses transform and opacity for GPU-composited animations
Includes prefers-reduced-motion fallback for all micro-interactions
Card interactions feel intentional — not generic shadow-lift on hover

query: I'm building a new SaaS product. The brand color is #E84855 (a warm red). Generate a complete color system from this, and design a component system with consistent spacing, border-radius, and shadow scales. React + Tailwind.
assertions: Announces it is using the frontend-design skill
Generates a multi-shade palette (50-950 or similar scale) from the brand color
Describes the palette generation method used (HSL manipulation, OKLCH, complementary, etc.)
Generates complementary/analogous secondary and accent colors with rationale
Defines a spacing scale for component padding (e.g., compact/default/spacious)
Defines a border-radius scale (e.g., sm/md/lg/full) with specific values
Defines a shadow scale (e.g., sm/md/lg/xl) with specific values tied to elevation
Shows how these scales compose in actual components (button sizes, card variants)
